D1 Optics L2 Understand Atom

Plane Mirror Image

drag an object in front of a mirror and watch its virtual twin appear at equal distance behind

A plane mirror forms a virtual image at the same perpendicular distance behind it as the object is in front — the simplest case of geometric imaging.

Slide the object distance s. The virtual image follows exactly behind the mirror at s' = s. Gold rays bounce off the mirror surface; dashed gold rays back-trace to the virtual position.
